title: Methods and compositions for determining ploidy
abstract: The invention provides improved methods, compositions, and kits for detecting ploidy of chromosome regions, e.g. for detecting cancer or a chromosomal abnormality in a gestating fetus. The methods can utilize a set of more than 200 SNPs that are found within haploblocks and can include analyzing a series of target chromosomal regions related to cancer or a chromosomal abnormality in a gestating fetus. Finally the method may use knowledge about chromosome crossover locations or a best fit algorithm for the analysis. The compositions may comprise more than 200 primers located within haplotype blocks known to show CNV.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for determining the copy number of a chromosome or chromosome region of interest in a target individual, the method comprising: a. making genotypic measurements for a set of SNPs comprising 200 SNPs on the chromosome or chromosome region of interest from a sample of blood, or a fraction thereof from the target individual, wherein at least 90% of the SNPs from the set of SNPs have strong linkage disequilibrium with at least one other SNP of the set of SNPs; b. estimating the phase of the genotypic measurements; and c. determining on a computer, the likelihood of different ploidy states of the chromosome or chromosome region of interest by comparing the phased genotypic measurements to a set of joint distribution models of expected genetic measurements for different ploidy states using identified chromosome crossover locations, thereby determining the ploidy state as the copy number of the chromosome or chromosome region with the highest likelihood. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the determining is performed by: a. creating, on a computer, a set of ploidy state hypothesis where each ploidy state hypothesis is one possible ploidy state of the chromosome or chromosome region of interest; b. building a set of joint distribution models of expected genotypic measurements at the set of SNPs for each hypothesis considering identified chromosome crossover locations; and c. determining, on the computer, the likelihood of each of the hypotheses given the estimated phase of the genotypic measurements and the joint distribution model. 3. A method according to claim 1, wherein the step of making genotypic measurements is performed by measuring signal intensities for different alleles for each of the SNPs using a SNP microarray or by allele frequency measurements using high throughput sequencing. 4. A method according to claim 1, wherein the step of making genotypic measurements is performed by amplifying genetic material in the sample, using a pool of primers comprising at least 200 primers or primer pairs, and quantifying each allele at a S P loci of the set of S Ps. 5. A method for determining a copy number of a chromosome or chromosomal region of interest in a sample of an individual, the method comprising: a. receiving allele frequency data for each SNP of a set of SNPs comprising 200 SNPs on a plurality of segments within the chromosomal region, wherein each segment comprises loci with strong linkage disequilibrium, wherein the allele frequency data comprises the amount of each allele present in the sample at each loci; b. estimating the phase of the allele frequency data taking into account an increased statistical correlation of polymorphic loci within the same segment; c. generating individual likelihoods of allele frequencies for the polymorphic loci for different ploidy states using the allele frequency data; d. generating joint likelihoods for the plurality of linked polymorphic loci using the individual likelihoods and the phased allele frequency data; and e. selecting, based on the joint likelihoods, a best fit model indicative of chromosomal copy number, thereby determining the copy number of the chromosome or chromosome region. 6. A method according to claim 5, wherein receiving allele frequency data comprises receiving nucleic acid sequencing data for at least 200 different amplicons spanning each loci of the plurality of polymorphic loci and generating the allele frequency data from the sequencing data. 7. A method according to claim 6, further comprising a. amplifying the plurality of polymorphic loci by an amplification method comprising: i. forming a reaction mixture comprising circulating free nucleic acids derived from the sample, a polymerase and a pool of primers comprising at least 200 primers or primer pairs that each specifically bind to a primer binding sequence located within an effective distance of one of the polymorphic loci; and 

 ii. subjecting the reaction mixture to amplification conditions, thereby generating a plurality of amplicons; and b. subjecting each of the amplicons to a nucleic acid sequencing reaction to generate the nucleic acid sequencing data for the amplicons. 8. A method according claim 7, wherein a beta binomial distribution is used to determine individual likelihoods of allele frequencies for the polymorphic loci for different ploidy states. 9. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8. wherein a confidence is computed for the copy number determination. 10. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8. further comprising obtaining prior likelihoods of each hypothesis from population data, and computing the confidence using Bayesian estimation. 11. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, further comprising correcting the genotypic measurements or allele frequency data for bias, contamination, and/or sequencing errors. 12. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein an average allelic imbalance is calculated, and wherein the copy number determination is indicative of a copy number variation if the average allelic imbalance is equal to or greater than 0.45%. 13. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein a likelihood for each ploidy state is determined based on a beta binomial distribution of expected and observed genotypic measurements or allelic frequency data at the set of S Ps. 14. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the determining step comprises determining the ploidy state with the highest likelihood based on Bayesian estimation, as an indication of the number of copies of the chromosome or chromosome region of interest. 15. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the sample is the only sample whose phase is estimated for the individual. 16. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the set of S Ps comprises 1,000 S Ps. 17. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, further comprising correcting the genotypic measurements or allele frequency data for bias, contamination, and/or sequencing errors and obtaining prior likelihoods of each hypothesis from population data, and computing the confidence using Bayes Rule, wherein a confidence is computed for the copy number determination. 18. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, further comprising determining the ploidy state using a quantitative, non-allelic method, wherein an identification of the chromosome or chromosome region of interest as aneuploid by the quantitative, non-allelic method or by the method of the preceding claims, is indicative of a copy number variation for the chromosome or chromosome region of interest. 19. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8. wherein the quantitative, non-allelic method comprises: (a) measuring the amount of genetic material on each of the chromosome(s) or chromosome region(s) of interest; (b) comparing the measured amounts of genetic material for each of the chromosome(s) or chromosome regions from step (a) against each other; and (c) detecting the CNV or aneuoploidy by identifying the presence or absence of a deletion or duplication of at least one of the chromosomes or chromosome regions of interest based on the comparison(s). 20. A method according to claim 19, wherein the quantitative, non-allelic method uses data generated from the same sample that was used for the genotypic measurements and/or allele frequency data. 21. A method according to claim 7, wherein the amplification method is a PCR reaction and the annealing temperature is between 1 and 15 Â°C greater than the melting temperature of at least 50% of the primers of the set of primers. 22. A method according to claim 21, wherein the multiplex amplification reaction is performed under limiting primer conditions. 23. A method for amplifying a set of target nucleic acids within a chromosome or chromosome region of interest of an individual, wherein the method comprises: a. forming a reaction mixture comprising circulating free nucleic acids derived from a sample of blood or a fragment thereof of the individual, a polymerase and a pool of primers that comprises at least 500 primers or primer pairs wherein at least 90% of the primers or primer pairs in the reaction mixture specifically bind to a primer binding sequence located within one or more of a plurality of haploblocks found within the chromosome region, wherein the chromosome region is known to exhibit copy number variation (CNV) associated with a disorder or disease; and b. subjecting the reaction mixture to amplification conditions to generate a plurality of amplicons, thereby amplifying the set of target nucleic acids. 24. A method according to claim 23, wherein each of the primer binding sequences is located an effective distance from a SNP of a set of SNPs comprising 200 SNPs on the chromosome or chromosome region of interest. 25. A method according to claims 1-8 and 24, wherein at least 50% of the SNPs from the set of SNPs have strong linkage disequilibrium with at least one other SNP of the set of SNPs. 26. A method according to claims 1-8 and 24, wherein at least 90% of the SNPs from the set of SNPs have strong linkage disequilibrium with at least one other SNP of the set of SNPs. 27. A method according to claim 26, wherein the set of SNPs comprises 1000 SNPs. 28. A method according to claims 1-8 and 23, wherein the chromosome or chromosome region of interest is a chromosome region of 50 megabases or less. 29. A method according to claims 28, wherein at least 90% of the primers or primer pairs of the pool of primers bind to a primer binding sequence located within a haploblock of a plurality of haploblocks. 30. A method according to claim 29, wherein each haploblock of the plurality of haploblocks comprises at least 2 S Ps. 31. A method according to claims 1-8 and 23, wherein the chromosome or chromosome region of interest is a chromosome region of greater than 50 megabases. 32. A method according to claim 31, wherein at least 90% of the primers or primer pairs of the pool of primers bind to a primer binding sequence located within a haploblock of a plurality of haploblocks and wherein the pool of primers comprises at least 1000 primers or primer pairs. 33. A method according to claim 31, wherein each haploblock of the plurality of haploblocks comprises at least 10 SNPs. 34. A method according to claims 7 or 23, wherein the amplification method is a PCR reaction, the annealing temperature is between 1 and 15 Â°C greater than the melting temperature of at least 50% of the primers of the set of primers, the length of the annealing step in the PCR reaction is between 15 and 120 minutes, the primer concentration in the amplification reaction is between 1 and 10 nM, and the primers in the set of primers, are designed to minimize primer dimer formation. 35. A method according to claim 33, wherein each haploblock of the plurality comprises between 10 and 100 SNPs. 36. A method according to claims 4, 7, or 23, wherein each primer or primer pair binds an effective distance from a S P for amplifying the S P, wherein at least 50% of the S Ps are found within a gene having a CNV associated with cancer, and wherein each SNP is located within a haploblock of a plurality of haploblocks comprising at least 5 SNPs. 37. A method according to claim 23, wherein the method further comprises subjecting the amplicons to a sequencing reaction to generate sequencing data that maps to the human genome and optionally sequencing data that does not map to the human genome, and wherein at least 90% of the sequencing data maps to the human genome within a haploblock. 38. A method according to claim 23, wherein the method further comprises subjecting the amplicons to a sequencing reaction to generate sequencing data that maps to the human genome and optionally sequencing data that does not map to the human genome, and wherein at least 90%) of the sequencing data that maps to the human genome comprises a SNP of a set of SNPs that exhibits strong linkage disequilibrium to another SNP of the set of SNPs. 39. A method according to any one of claim 1-8 and 23, wherein the pool of primers amplify amplicons across 1000 to 50,000 polymeric loci on a set of chromosomes or chromosome regions of interest. 40. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8 and 23, wherein the individual is a fetus and the chromosome or chromosome region of interest is selected from the group consisting of chromosome 13, chromosome 18, chromosome 21, the X chromosome, the Y chromosome, and the combinations thereof. 41. A method according to claim 40, wherein a fetal fraction is less than 20%. 42. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8 and 23, wherein the individual is an individual suspected of having cancer, and the sample comprises ctDNA. 43. A method according to claim 42, wherein ctDNA is between 1%> and 20% of the cfDNA. 42. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 8 and 23, wherein the method is used to determine the copy number of at least 2 chromosomes and/or chromosome regions of interest. 43. A method according to any one of claims 1-8 and 23, wherein the set of S Ps further comprises a plurality of SNPs from at least one chromosome that is expected to be disomic. 44. A method according to claim 43, wherein the chromosome whose ploidy state is to be determined and the at least one chromosome that is expected to be disomic overlap. 45. A composition, comprising a pool of primers, comprising 200 primers or primer pairs that each specifically bind to a primer binding sequence located within one or more of a plurality of haploblocks found within a chromosome region known to exhibit copy number variation (CNV) associated with a disorder or disease, wherein each haploblock comprises at least 2 of the primer binding sequences and wherein at least 90% of the primer binding sequences are located within haploblocks. 46. A composition according to claim 45, wherein composition comprises 1000 primers or primer pairs. 47. A composition according to any one of claims 45 or 46, wherein at least 95% of the primer binding sequences are located within haploblocks. 48. A composition according to claim 47, wherein each haploblock comprises at least 10 of the primer binding sequences. 49. A reaction mixture, comprising: a. a population of circulating free nucleic acids from an individual, or nucleic acid fragments derived therefrom, and b. a composition comprising 200 primers or primer pairs that each specifically bind to a primer binding sequence located within one or more of a plurality of 

 haploblocks found within a chromosome region known to exhibit copy number variation (CNV) associated with a disorder or disease, wherein each haploblock comprises at least 2 of the primer binding sequences and wherein at least 90% of the primer binding sequences are located within haploblocks. 50. A reaction mixture of claim 49, wherein the circulating free nucleic acids comprising circulating tumor nucleic acids and the chromosome region is known to exhibit CNV associated with cancer. 51. A reaction mixture according to any one of claim 49 or 50, wherein the composition comprises 1000 primes. 52. A reaction mixture according to any one of claims 49 or 50, wherein the reaction mixture is an amplification reaction wherein the primer concentration in the amplification reaction mixture is between 1 and 10 nM, and the primers in the set of primers are designed to minimize primer dimer formation. 53. A method according to any one of claim 1 to 8, or 23, wherein the chromosome or chromosome region of interest is 50 megabases or less and comprises a gene targeted by an identified therapeutic, and wherein the sample comprises ctDNA. 54. A method according to any one of claim 1 to 8, or 23, wherein the chromosome or chromosome region of interest is a series of chromosome or chromosome regions, each 20 megabases or less and each comprising one of a series of genes each targeted by an identified therapeutic, and wherein the sample comprises ctDNA.
